DATuM
  • transformations.py
  • View page source

transformations.py

Provides functions for transforming the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data from the local PIV coordinate system to the global Cartesian coordinate system of the corresponding BeVERLI Hill experiment in the Virginia Tech Stability Wind Tunnel.

datum.transformations.get_rotation_matrix(rotation_angle_deg, rotation_axis)

Gets the rotation matrix for an Euler rotation of a body about a specified axis of its Cartesian coordinate system.

Parameters:

  • rotation_angle_deg (float) – Rotation angle measured in degrees.

  • rotation_axis (Tuple[float, float, float]) – 3D vector components of the axis of rotation as a tuple of shape (3, ).

Returns:

Rotation matrix as ndarray of shape (3, 3).

Return type:

ndarray

datum.transformations.calculate_unit_vector(data)

Normalizes an input vector.

Parameters:

data (Tuple[float, float, float]) – 3D vector components of an input vector.

Returns:

The normalized vector as ndarray of shape (3, ).

Return type:

ndarray

datum.transformations.rotate_data(piv_obj)

Rotates the original BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data from its local PIV coordinate system to the Cartesian coordinate system used in the Virginia Tech Stability Wind Tunnel.

The original data comprises coordinates, mean velocity, Reynolds stress tensor, and an instantaneous velocity frame (if available). Additional derived quantities, such as the mean rate-of-strain tensor, are computed directly in the (rotated) coordinate system of the Virginia Tech Stability Wind Tunnel.

If you need to rotate a specific flow quantity to a coordinate system not handled by this routine, consider the datum.transformations.rotate_flow_quantity() function.

The present routine directly edits the Piv object that is passed to it.

Parameters:

piv_obj – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.rotate_flow_quantity(piv_obj, quantity, components, rotation_matrix)

Rotates a specified BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV flow quantity using a specified rotation matrix.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Object containing the BeVERLI Hill stereo PIV data.

  • quantity (str) – String specifying the flow quantity to rotate.

  • components (List[str]) – A list of strings specifying the components of the vector or tensor quantity to rotate.

  • rotation_matrix (ndarray) – The rotation matrix used to rotate the data as ndarray of shape (3, 3).

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.rotate_vector_quantity(vector, rotation_matrix)

Rotate a BeVERLI stereo PIV vector quantity based on the provided rotation matrix.

This method takes a rotation matrix and a list of vector component matrices as input. It then calculates the rotated quantity and returns it.

Parameters:

  • rotation_matrix (ndarray) – NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3) representing the rotation matrix.

  • vector (ndarray) – List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) containing the rotated vector quantity’s components, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

datum.transformations.rotate_tensor_quantity(tensor, rotation_matrix)

Rotate a BeVERLI stereo PIV tensor quantity based on the provided rotation matrix.

This method takes a rotation matrix and a list of tensor component matrices as input. It then calculates the rotated quantity and returns it.

Parameters:

  • rotation_matrix (ndarray) – NumPy ndarray of shape (3, 3) representing the rotation matrix.

  • tensor (ndarray) – List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) containing the rotated vector quantity’s components, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

datum.transformations.prepare_vector_quantity_for_rotation(piv_obj, quantity, components)

Build the vector of a desired quantity from the corresponding components of the BeVERLI stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the class datum.piv.Piv class.

  • quantity (str) – String specifying the vector quantity.

  • components (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the vector components.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each containing a specific vector component, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

datum.transformations.prepare_tensor_quantity_for_rotation(piv_obj, quantity, components)

Build the tensor of a desired quantity from the corresponding components of the BeVERLI stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

  • quantity (str) – String specifying the tensor quantity.

  • components (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the tensor components.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), containing specific tensor components, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

datum.transformations.set_rotated_vector_quantity(piv_obj, quantity, components, rotated_vector)

Update the BeVERLI stereo PIV data with the rotated vector.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

  • quantity (str) – String specifying the vector quantity.

  • components (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the vector components.

  • rotated_vector (ndarray) – List of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n), each representing a vector component, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.set_rotated_tensor_quantity(piv_obj, quantity, components, rotated_tensor)

Update the BeVERLI stereo PIV data with the rotated tensor.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

  • quantity (str) – String specifying the tensor quantity.

  • components (List[str]) – List of strings specifying the tensor components.

  • rotated_tensor (ndarray) – List of lists of NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n). Each array represents a tensor component, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.interpolate_data(piv_obj)

Perform a comprehensive routine to interpolate the BeVERLI stereo PIV data to a dense grid within the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Parameters:

piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.get_interpolation_grid(piv_obj)

Build a structured, dense coordinate mesh for the interpolation of the BeVERLI stereo PIV data.

Parameters:

piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

Return type:

Tuple[ndarray, ndarray]

Returns:

A tuple containing NumPy ndarrays of shape (m, n) that represent a mesh grid, where m and n represent the number of available data points in the x:sub:1- and x:sub:2-direction.

datum.transformations.get_flattened_coordinates(piv_obj)

Retrieve an array of flattened spatial coordinates from the BeVERLI stereo PIV data, necessary for subsequent interpolation.

Parameters:

piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

NumPy ndarray of shape (m, 2) containing the flattened set of spatial coordinates for the desired quantity from the stereo PIV data, where m represents the number of total available data points.

datum.transformations.get_flattened_data(piv_obj, data_type, key)

Retrieve an array of flattened data of a specific vector or tensor quantity from the stereo PIV data, necessary for subsequent interpolation.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

  • data_type (str) – A string specifying the desired quantity to interpolate.

  • key (str) – A string indicating the desired vector or tensor component.

Return type:

ndarray

Returns:

NumPy ndarrays of shape (n, ) containing the flattened set of values for the desired quantity’s component from the stereo PIV data, where n represents the number of total available data points.

datum.transformations.set_interpolated_data(piv_obj, data_type, key, interpolated_data)

Update the PIV data with the interpolated data.

Parameters:

  • piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

  • data_type (str) – A string specifying the particular vector or tensor quantity to update.

  • key (str) – A string specifying the particular component of the vector or tensor quantity to update.

  • interpolated_data (ndarray) – NumPy ndarrays of shape (n, ) representing the interpolated component, where n represents the number of total available data points.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.translate_data(piv_obj)

Perform a comprehensive routine to translate the BeVERLI stereo PIV data from its local to the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.get_translation_vector(piv_obj)

Obtain the translation vector from the available coordinate transformation parameters.

Parameters:

piv_obj – Instance of the datum.piv.Piv class.

Return type:

Tuple[float, float]

Returns:

A tuple of floats, representing the components of the translation vector.

datum.transformations.translate_coordinates(coordinates, x_1_shift, x_2_shift)

Perform the coordinate translation from the local PIV to the global BeVERLI coordinate system.

Parameters:

  • coordinates (Dict[str, ndarray]) – Dictionary containing the stereo PIV coordinates.

  • x_1_shift (float) – Coordinate shift in the x_1 direction.

  • x_2_shift (float) – Coordinate shift in the x_2 direction.

Return type:

None

datum.transformations.scale_coordinates(piv_obj, scale_factor)
Return type:

None

datum.transformations.rotate_profile(profile, rotation_matrix)
datum.transformations.rotate_tensor_profile(tensor, rotation_matrix)
datum.transformations.set_rotated_profiles(profile, velocity_vector_rotated, re_stress_tensor_rotated, strain_tensor_rotated=None, rotation_tensor_rotated=None, normalized_rotation_tensor_rotated=None)
